pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 16 (EXPORT_DIR definition), line 22 (file reference), export_result.sh line 19 (EXPORT_FILE definition), and task description section 4
Task description specifies field mappings: STN_ID_X→station_id, LOC_NM→name, READ_VAL→temperature, OBS_DT→date. Verifier.py lines 62-65 confirm these exact field names required in output.

Setup_task.sh lines 32-43 define source data with these exact field names. Export_result.sh line 68 forbidden list matches exactly. Task description requires renaming these fields (STN_ID_X→station_id, LOC_NM→name, READ_VAL→temperature) or deleting them (LEGACY_CD, TECH_N).

Setup_task.sh lines 32-43 contains exactly 10 GeoJSON feature objects (STN001, STN002, STN003, STN004, STN005, STN006, STN007, STN008, STN009, STN010). Python JSON parsing confirmed feature count = 10.

pi_items.metadata_value: Temperature field must be converted from String to Double/Real type
pi_items.verified_value: In source data (setup_task.sh), READ_VAL is string type with values: '12.5', '13.2', '11.8', '12.0', '14.1', '10.9', '11.5', '13.0', '9.8', '12.2'. Verifier (line 73) checks: isinstance(temp_val, (int, float)) must be True.
pi_items.source: Task description section 3 specifies 'change the type from String to Double/Real (Precision 1 or 2)'. Verifier.py line 75-79 awards 25 points for this conversion being correct.

pi_items.verified_value: Entirely synthetic data generated by setup_task.sh at runtime. No external data sources.
pi_items.source: Setup_task.sh lines 27-45: GeoJSON is generated inline using cat heredoc syntax with hardcoded feature coordinates and properties. No file downloads or external API calls.

pi_items.verified_value: London, England area. All 10 features have coordinates around latitude 51.48-51.53 (London area) and longitude -0.14 to -0.08.
pi_items.source: Setup_task.sh lines 33-43 contain coordinates for all features. Example: North Point [-0.12, 51.50], South Quay [-0.11, 51.49], etc.
Setup_task.sh lines 33-43: OBS_DT values range from '2023-01-01' (STN001) to '2023-01-10' (STN010), one per day.

pi_items.verified_value: 9.8 to 14.1 degrees (Celsius assumed, typical for London in January)
pi_items.source: Setup_task.sh lines 33-43: READ_VAL (temperature) values extracted: 12.5, 13.2, 11.8, 12.0, 14.1, 10.9, 11.5, 13.0, 9.8, 12.2. Min: 9.8 (STN009), Max: 14.1 (STN005).

pi_items.verified_value: LEGACY_CD contains three unique values: X99 (4 occurrences), Y01 (3 occurrences), Z22 (3 occurrences). TECH_N contains varied notes: 'Routine check', 'Bat changed', 'OK', 'Sensor drift', 'Calibrated', 'No access', 'Flooded', 'Windy'.
pi_items.source: Setup_task.sh lines 33-43: Parsed all 10 features to extract LEGACY_CD and TECH_N values.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses synthetic data: 10 weather station records (STN001-STN010) from London area with observations from 2023-01-01 to 2023-01-10. Source data contains 6 fields (STN_ID_X, LOC_NM, READ_VAL, OBS_DT, LEGACY_CD, TECH_N) that must be refactored to 4 fields (station_id, name, temperature, date) by renaming and type conversion. Temperature values range 9.8-14.1 degrees and must be converted from string to numeric type. Two fields (LEGACY_CD, TECH_N) must be deleted. The output should be saved to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/clean_stations.geojson with exactly 10 records.
